rationale: This page contains an introductory 'Note to Students' and a copyright notice. It outlines the features of the PT 365 document but does not contain substantive international relations content for question generation.
cleanedText:
Note to Students
Dear Students,
International Relations PT 365 document comprehensively covers the IR-related important current affairs events from April 2025 to November 2025 in a consolidated manner to aid Prelims preparation.
In our endeavour to further enhance the document in the interest of aspirants, the following additions have been incorporated:
- Theme-wise Organisation: Content has been systematically arranged under multilateral organisations, bilateral relations, places in news, security issues, and other important developments to enable integrated understanding.
- Prelims-Focused Fact Enrichment: Key facts, memberships, headquarters, mandates, summits, declarations, treaties, and agreements have been highlighted for quick recall.
- Maps and Location-Based Inputs: Extensive coverage of places in news, conflict zones, strategic corridors, seas, straits, and islands to aid map-based and geography-linked IR questions.
- India-Centric Perspective: Special emphasis has been placed on India’s bilateral engagements, strategic interests, and foreign policy priorities.
- Reports and Indices: Important global reports, indices, and initiatives by international organisations have been consolidated for rapid revision.
- Smart Revision Tools: QR-based quizzes have been included to test conceptual clarity and reinforce learning in an engaging manner.
Best Wishes,
Team Current Affairs
Team VisionIAS
